data.command: export PATH="$HOME/go/bin:$HOME/bin:$PATH" && gt prime --hook && gt mail check --inject

message.content:
[GAS TOWN] deacon <- daemon • 2026-02-23T17:02 • patrol

I am Deacon. Start patrol: run gt deacon heartbeat, then check gt hook. If no hook, create mol-deacon-patrol wisp and execute it.

content.content:
? skogtown-wisp-wisp-14nhp [EPIC] · mol-deacon-patrol   [● P2 · HOOKED]
Assignee: deacon · Type: epic
Created: 2026-02-22 · Updated: 2026-02-22

DESCRIPTION
Deacon patrol cycle.

Monitors Gas Town agents (Mayor, Witnesses), processes lifecycle requests,
and handles escalations. Created as wisps (ephemeral, not synced to git).

CHILDREN
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-4j4mo: Check for orphaned processes ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-5l7kf: Scan agent health ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-r7wub: Decide: loop or hand off ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-wewa9: Check inbox for lifecycle requests and messages ● P2

content.text: All children of this patrol wisp are already closed. This is a completed patrol. I'll squash it and start a fresh one.

input.command: gt mol squash --jitter 10s --summary "Previous patrol cycle complete: all steps closed"

content.thinking:
The `gt mol squash` command is crashing with a Dolt nil pointer dereference. This is a known issue with the embedded Dolt database. Let me try a different approach - just create a new patrol wisp directly since the old one's steps are all closed.

Let me try creating a new patrol directly.

content.text: Dolt crash on squash - known issue with embedded DB. The old patrol is fully closed anyway. I'll skip the squash and create a fresh patrol.
